---
title: "PFM Results — Price Stringency Model (`r params$reportName`)"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')`"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 1
    toc_float:
      collapsed: true
    number_sections: true
    code_folding: hide
params:
  modelConfig: "results/exhaustive/selected-models.yml"
  reportName: "default"
  gdxPath: "data/fulldata.gdx"
  cachefolder: ""
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
                      fig.width = 10, fig.height = 5.5)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(magclass)
library(pfm)

root <- getwd()
library(pfmreports)
library(pfmreports)
library(pfmreports)

if (nzchar(params$cachefolder)) madrat::setConfig(cachefolder = params$cachefolder)
madrat::setConfig(forcecache = TRUE)
model_dir <- getPfmConfig("modelDir", "../output")
if (!is_absolute_path(model_dir)) model_dir <- file.path(root, model_dir)
options(pfm.modelDir = model_dir)

cfg_path <- params$modelConfig
if (!is_absolute_path(cfg_path)) cfg_path <- file.path(root, cfg_path)
all_cfg <- yaml::read_yaml(cfg_path)
norm_cfg <- function(cfg) {
  for (f in c("actorPowerDrivers", "actorPowerIndex", "instQualityDrivers", "controlDrivers"))
    if (!is.null(cfg[[f]])) cfg[[f]] <- unlist(cfg[[f]])
  cfg$panelTransform <- cfg$panelTransform %||% "levels"
  cfg
}
sectors <- c("Bulk", "Diffuse")
get_cfg <- function(stage) {
  out <- list()
  for (sec in sectors) {
    hit <- Filter(function(x) identical(x$model_type, paste0(stage, ": ", sec)), all_cfg)
    out[[sec]] <- if (length(hit) > 0) norm_cfg(hit[[1]]) else NULL
  }
  for (sec in sectors) if (is.null(out[[sec]])) out[[sec]] <- out[[setdiff(sectors, sec)]]
  out
}
cfgS <- get_cfg("Stringency")
cfgA <- get_cfg("Adoption") # used only for the hurdle expectation
if (all(vapply(cfgS, is.null, logical(1)))) stop("No 'Stringency: <Sector>' entries in ", cfg_path)

hist_mag <- getPanelDataHistoricalCached(
  aggregate = TRUE, y = 2000:2022, outputRegionMappingFile = "regionmapping_54.csv"
)
fits <- list()
for (sec in sectors) {
  c0 <- cfgS[[sec]]
  fits[[sec]] <- estimatePriceStringencyModel(
    data = hist_mag, sector = sec, family = "gaussian",
    actorPowerDrivers = c0$actorPowerDrivers, actorPowerIndex = c0$actorPowerIndex,
    instQualityDrivers = c0$instQualityDrivers, controlDrivers = c0$controlDrivers,
    includeLaggedECP = isTRUE(c0$includeLaggedECP) || isTRUE(c0$includeLagged),
    interactRegionFE = isTRUE(c0$interactRegionFE),
    regionMappingFixedEffects = c0$regionMappingFixedEffects,
    useMundlak = isTRUE(c0$useMundlak), gdpGovInteraction = isTRUE(c0$gdpGovInteraction),
    logisticTimeTrend = isTRUE(c0$logisticTimeTrend),
    panelTransform = c0$panelTransform,
    nickellCorrection = isTRUE(c0$nickellCorrection),
    priceLink = c0$priceLink %||% "log1p",
    priceCeilingMax = c0$priceCeilingMax %||% 1000,
    modelDir = model_dir, verbose = FALSE
  )
}

# ── Scenario projections: stringency price x adoption probability ─────────────
gdx_path <- params$gdxPath
if (nzchar(gdx_path) && !is_absolute_path(gdx_path)) gdx_path <- file.path(root, gdx_path)
scen_mag <- NULL
if (nzchar(gdx_path) && file.exists(gdx_path)) {
  scen_mag <- tryCatch(
    getPanelDataScenarioCached(gdxFile = gdx_path, aggregate = TRUE,
                               outputRegionMappingFile = "regionmapping_54.csv"),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
}
proj <- list()
if (!is.null(scen_mag)) {
  for (sec in sectors) {
    pS <- tryCatch(projectSpecScenario(cfgS[[sec]], sec, hist_mag, scen_mag,
                                       modelDir = model_dir), error = function(e) NULL)
    pA <- if (!is.null(cfgA[[sec]])) {
      tryCatch(projectSpecScenario(cfgA[[sec]], sec, hist_mag, scen_mag,
                                   modelDir = model_dir), error = function(e) NULL)
    } else NULL
    if (!is.null(pS)) {
      out <- pS %>% select(region, year, sector, price)
      if (!is.null(pA)) {
        out <- merge(out, pA %>% select(region, year, prob), by = c("region", "year"))
        out$expectedPrice <- out$prob * out$price
      } else {
        out$prob <- NA_real_
        out$expectedPrice <- out$price
      }
      proj[[sec]] <- out
    }
  }
}
has_proj <- length(proj) > 0
```

# Executive Summary

```{r exec, results='asis'}
for (sec in sectors) {
  f <- fits[[sec]]
  pd <- f$predictiveDiagnostics
  cat(sprintf(paste0(
    "- **%s** — `%s` | panelTransform: %s | family: %s | N = %d | maxVIF: %.1f | ",
    "in-sample RMSE (estimation scale): %s | obs-fit correlation: %s\n"
  ), sec, cfgS[[sec]]$name, cfgS[[sec]]$panelTransform, f$family,
     length(f$model$y), f$maxVIF %||% NA,
     if (!is.null(pd)) sprintf("%.3f", pd$rmse) else "—",
     if (!is.null(pd)) sprintf("%.3f", pd$corObsPred) else "—"))
}
cat(sprintf("\nHurdle projections (adoption-gated expected price): %s\n",
            if (has_proj) "available (SSP2)" else "not available"))
```

# Model Equations & Coefficients {.tabset}

```{r coefs, results='asis', fig.height=6}
for (sec in sectors) {
  cat(sprintf("## %s\n\n", sec))
  f <- fits[[sec]]
  ct <- as.data.frame(unclass(f$coeftest))
  names(ct) <- c("estimate", "stdError", "zValue", "pValue")
  ct$term <- rownames(f$coeftest)
  ct$signif <- sigStars(ct$pValue)
  main <- ct %>% filter(!grepl("^regionFE", term))
  eq_terms <- sprintf("%+.3f·%s%s", main$estimate,
                      ifelse(main$term == "(Intercept)", "1", main$term), main$signif)
  cat(sprintf("```\nE[log(1+ECP)] = %s + [region FE]   (gaussian identity; price = expm1(.))\n```\n\n",
              paste(eq_terms, collapse = " ")))
  cc <- ct %>% filter(!grepl("^regionFE|^\\(Intercept", term)) %>%
    mutate(lo = estimate - 1.96 * stdError, hi = estimate + 1.96 * stdError,
           sig = pValue < 0.05)
  p <- ggplot(cc, aes(estimate, reorder(term, estimate), colour = sig)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
    geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = lo, xmax = hi)) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c(`TRUE` = "#d62728", `FALSE` = "grey60")) +
    theme_report() + theme(legend.position = "none") +
    labs(title = paste0(sec, " — coefficients (robust 95% CI)"),
         x = "Estimate (per 1 SD of the driver)", y = NULL)
  print(p)
  cat("\n\n")
  print(knitr::kable(ct %>% select(term, estimate, stdError, zValue, pValue, signif) %>%
                       mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 3))),
                     caption = paste0(sec, " — full coefficient table (per-SD drivers)")))
  cat("\n\n")
}
howToRead("Stringency coefficients are on the log(1+ECP) scale (a log-linear model: ",
          "gaussian identity link on the log-transformed price, so E[log(1+ECP)] = Xβ ",
          "and price = expm1(Xβ)). Drivers are **standardized**, so each coefficient is ",
          "the effect of a **one-standard-deviation** increase in that driver — magnitudes ",
          "are directly comparable across drivers (the largest is the most influential). ",
          "To express a coefficient in natural units, divide it by that driver's SD. Read ",
          "direction and significance here; the next section gives real-world magnitudes.")
```

# Real-World Effects — Semi-Elasticities & USD Effects {.tabset}

```{r semi, results='asis'}
for (sec in sectors) {
  cat(sprintf("## %s\n\n", sec))
  f <- fits[[sec]]
  ct <- as.data.frame(unclass(f$coeftest))
  names(ct) <- c("estimate", "stdError", "zValue", "pValue")
  ct$term <- rownames(f$coeftest)
  meanFitted <- mean(expm1(as.numeric(fitted(f$model))), na.rm = TRUE)
  eff <- ct %>%
    filter(!grepl("^regionFE|^\\(Intercept", term)) %>%
    mutate(
      semiElasticityPct = round((exp(estimate) - 1) * 100, 1),
      effectUSDtCO2 = round((exp(estimate) - 1) * meanFitted, 2),
      signif = sigStars(pValue)
    ) %>%
    select(term, estimate, semiElasticityPct, effectUSDtCO2, pValue, signif)
  print(knitr::kable(eff %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 3))), caption = sprintf(
    "%s — effect per 1 SD of driver (mean fitted price %.1f USD/tCO2)", sec, meanFitted)))
  cat("\n\n")
}
howToRead("semiElasticityPct = (e^β − 1)·100: the % change in the (log1p) price index ",
          "per **one standard deviation** increase in the driver (drivers are standardized, ",
          "so effects are comparable across drivers). effectUSDtCO2 translates that to ",
          "dollars at the mean fitted price — a rough but intuitive 'so what' number. ",
          "For a natural-unit effect, rescale by the driver's SD.",
          type = "table")
```

# Historical Fit & Outliers {.tabset}

## Calibration (45°)

```{r calib, fig.height=5}
cal <- bind_rows(lapply(sectors, function(sec) {
  f <- fits[[sec]]
  data.frame(sector = sec,
             observed = as.numeric(f$model$y),
             fitted = as.numeric(fitted(f$model)))
}))
ggplot(cal, aes(fitted, observed)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, colour = "grey50", linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, colour = "#1f77b4", size = 1.2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, colour = "#d62728", linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~sector) +
  theme_report() +
  labs(title = "Observed vs fitted (estimation scale: log(1+ECP))",
       x = "Fitted", y = "Observed")
```

```{r calib-cap, results='asis'}
howToRead("Each dot is an adopting region-year on the estimation scale (log(1+ECP) for ",
          "levels specs, Δlog(1+ECP) for FD). Perfect calibration = dots on the dashed ",
          "45° line; the red regression line bending away from it shows where the model ",
          "systematically over/under-predicts price levels.")
```

## Price trajectories (USD/tCO2)

```{r traj, fig.height=5}
tr <- bind_rows(lapply(sectors, function(sec) {
  f <- fits[[sec]]
  idx <- usedRows(f) # fitted values align to complete-case rows only
  df <- f$data[idx, , drop = FALSE]
  data.frame(sector = sec, region = df$region, year = df$year,
             observed = expm1(as.numeric(f$model$y)),
             fitted = expm1(as.numeric(fitted(f$model))))
}))
ci <- capAtP99(c(tr$observed, tr$fitted))
trL <- tr %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(c(observed, fitted), names_to = "kind", values_to = "price") %>%
  mutate(price = pmin(price, ci$cap))
ggplot(trL, aes(year, price, colour = kind, group = interaction(region, kind))) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.35, linewidth = 0.35) +
  facet_wrap(~sector) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(observed = "#1f77b4", fitted = "#d62728")) +
  theme_report() +
  labs(title = "Observed vs fitted carbon prices (adopting region-years)",
       x = NULL, y = "USD/tCO2", colour = NULL, subtitle = capAnnotation(ci))
```

```{r traj-cap, results='asis'}
howToRead("Back-transformed to USD/tCO2 (display-capped at p99 — Price Outlier rule). ",
          "Blue = observed, red = model fit, one line per adopting region. Persistent ",
          "red-above-blue (or below) for a region indicates a level bias the region FE ",
          "did not absorb.")
```

## Price outliers

```{r outliers, results='asis'}
obs_pos <- tr$observed[is.finite(tr$observed) & tr$observed > 0]
p99 <- as.numeric(quantile(obs_pos, 0.99, na.rm = TRUE))
out_rows <- tr %>%
  filter(observed > p99 | fitted > p99) %>%
  mutate(across(c(observed, fitted), ~round(.x, 1))) %>%
  arrange(desc(pmax(observed, fitted)))
cat(sprintf("**Price Outlier threshold (p99 of positive historical ECP): %.1f USD/tCO2.**\n\n", p99))
if (nrow(out_rows) > 0) {
  interactiveTable(out_rows, caption = "Region-years above the p99 price threshold (observed or fitted)")
} else cat("*No outliers above p99.*\n")
```

```{r outliers-cap, results='asis'}
howToRead("Every region-year whose observed or fitted price exceeds the p99 threshold. ",
          "These are kept in estimation (never dropped) but capped in charts; a fitted ",
          "value here without a matching observed one means the model invents extreme ",
          "prices for that region — check its drivers.", type = "table")
```

## Residual diagnostics

```{r resid, fig.height=4.5}
rd <- bind_rows(lapply(sectors, function(sec) {
  f <- fits[[sec]]
  data.frame(sector = sec,
             fitted = as.numeric(fitted(f$model)),
             residual = as.numeric(f$model$y) - as.numeric(fitted(f$model)))
}))
ggplot(rd, aes(fitted, residual)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey50", linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, colour = "#1f77b4", size = 1.1) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, colour = "#d62728", linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~sector) +
  theme_report() +
  labs(title = "Residuals vs fitted (estimation scale)", x = "Fitted", y = "Residual")
```

```{r resid-cap, results='asis'}
howToRead("Raw residuals against fitted values. A flat red smoother around zero is ",
          "healthy; funnels (heteroskedasticity) or curvature (missed nonlinearity) ",
          "show where the log-linear functional form struggles.")
```

# Future Projections (SSP2) {.tabset}

```{r proj, results='asis', fig.height=6}
if (has_proj) {
  pall <- bind_rows(proj)

  cat("## Hurdle-expected price trajectories\n\n")
  pp <- pall %>% mutate(expCapped = pmin(expectedPrice, 1000))
  p <- ggplot(pp, aes(year, expCapped, group = region)) +
    geom_line(alpha = 0.35, linewidth = 0.4, colour = "#1f77b4") +
    stat_summary(aes(group = 1), fun = median, geom = "line",
                 colour = "#d62728", linewidth = 1.2) +
    facet_wrap(~sector) +
    theme_report() +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1000)) +
    labs(title = "Projected expected carbon price: P(adoption) × price | adoption",
         x = NULL, y = "USD/tCO2", subtitle = "Capped at 1000 USD/tCO2")
  print(p)
  cat("\n\n")

  outliers <- pall %>%
    filter(expectedPrice > 1000) %>%
    mutate(expectedPrice = round(expectedPrice, 1)) %>%
    select(sector, region, year, expectedPrice) %>%
    arrange(desc(expectedPrice))
  if (nrow(outliers) > 0) {
    print(knitr::kable(outliers, caption = "Projected expected prices above 1000 USD/tCO2"))
  } else {
    cat("*No projected expected prices exceed 1000 USD/tCO2.*\n")
  }
  cat("\n\n")

  howToRead("The hurdle expectation per region: adoption probability times the ",
            "conditional price, back-transformed to USD/tCO2 and display-capped at 1000 USD/tCO2. ",
            "Red = median region. This is the quantity a REMIND rigidity factor would ",
            "be built from — shapes matter more than absolute levels.")

  cat("## Price ranking at snapshots\n\n")
  snap <- pall %>%
    filter(year %in% c(2030, 2050, 2070, 2100)) %>%
    mutate(expectedPrice = round(expectedPrice, 1)) %>%
    select(sector, region, year, expectedPrice) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = year, values_from = expectedPrice, names_prefix = "y")
  print(knitr::kable(snap %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 1))),
                     caption = "Hurdle-expected price (USD/tCO2) at snapshot years"))
  cat("\n\n")
  howToRead("Sortable per-region expected prices at four horizons — the ranking view ",
            "for 'which regions can sustain high carbon prices, when'. Values above the ",
            "2000 USD/tCO2 sanity threshold would be severe Projection Sanity flags.",
            type = "table")

  cat("## Projected price distribution\n\n")
  pv <- pall %>% filter(year %in% c(2030, 2050, 2070, 2100))
  pv$expectedPrice <- pmin(pv$expectedPrice, 1000)
  p2 <- ggplot(pv, aes(factor(year), expectedPrice)) +
    geom_violin(fill = "#1f77b4", alpha = 0.3, colour = "#1f77b4", scale = "width") +
    geom_boxplot(width = 0.15, outlier.size = 1, outlier.colour = "grey40", fill = "white", alpha = 0.6) +
    facet_wrap(~sector) +
    theme_report() +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1000)) +
    labs(title = "Distribution of expected prices across regions",
         x = NULL, y = "USD/tCO2", subtitle = "Capped at 1000 USD/tCO2")
  print(p2)
  cat("\n\n")
  howToRead("Cross-region distribution of the hurdle-expected price at each snapshot. ",
            "A widening violin = growing regional divergence in feasible carbon pricing; ",
            "the median line of the box is the 'typical region'.")
} else {
  cat("> Projections unavailable: no scenario panel, or the selected spec is an FD ",
      "transform (not projectable — ADR 0005).\n")
}
```
